essence: Monitors and enforces environmental standards to protect communities, ecosystems, and public health.

identity.title: Environmental Protection Officer
identity.summary: Investigates pollution, monitors environmental quality, enforces regulations, and works with businesses and communities to reduce environmental harm.

salary.notes: Specialists in air quality, water pollution, or industrial regulation can exceed £65k-£80k+.

qualifications: Training in environmental science or environmental health
Knowledge of pollution control and environmental legislation
Ability to conduct investigations and sampling
Understanding of ecosystems, waste management, and environmental risk

lifestyle.description: Environmental protection officers work for councils, environmental agencies, and regulatory bodies. They investigate pollution, monitor environmental quality, enforce regulations, and support sustainable practices. The role suits individuals who enjoy fieldwork, investigation, and protecting natural environments.
